sacco

Class SaccoWindow

  • java.lang.Object
    • sacco.SaccoWindow
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    DrawingBoard, KeyboardListener, MouseListener
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    ShapeOrganizer, Turtle

    public class SaccoWindow
extends java.lang.Object
implements DrawingBoard, MouseListener, KeyboardListener

      • onKeyPress

        public void onKeyPress(int keyCode)
        Description copied from interface: KeyboardListener
        Once a KeyboardListener is registered, this code will be called every time that a keyboard button is pressed.
        Specified by:
        onKeyPress in interface KeyboardListener
        Parameters:
        keyCode - - the keyCode of the key that was pressed

      • onKeyRelease

        public void onKeyRelease(int keyCode)
        Description copied from interface: KeyboardListener
        Once a KeyboardListener is registered, this code will be called every time that a keyboard button is released.
        Specified by:
        onKeyRelease in interface KeyboardListener
        Parameters:
        keyCode - - the keyCode of the key that was pressed

      • onMousePress

        public void onMousePress(MouseEvent m)
        Description copied from interface: MouseListener
        Once a MouseListener is registered, this method will be called every time a mouse button is pressed
        Specified by:
        onMousePress in interface MouseListener
        Parameters:
        m - - An object containing information about the event. Useful methods include m.getX() and m.getY()

      • onMouseRelease

        public void onMouseRelease(MouseEvent m)
        Description copied from interface: MouseListener
        Once a MouseListener is registered, this method will be called every time a mouse button is released
        Specified by:
        onMouseRelease in interface MouseListener
        Parameters:
        m - - An object containing information about the event. Useful methods include m.getX() and m.getY()

        Specified by:

        Parameters:
        m - - An object containing information about the event. Useful methods include m.getX() and m.getY()
